Company Short Description:
Amhil North America (formerly Amhil Enterprises) was established in 1975 by a couple of Dutch engineers from two neighboring cities in the Netherlands called Amsterdam and Hilversum. Together they launched Amhil, a privately held Thermoforming Plastics Packaging company focused on delivering Best Value to the North American Food Services market. In the early days, Amhil first entered the New York market, offering a variety of hot drink lids to restaurants and kiosks.

Joining the TFI International group in 1998 represents one of its key decisions. Since then, Transport J.C. Germain has borne the name TFI Transport 1 L.P., becoming one of the first major acquisitions of this publicly traded multinational.

Through the years, five well-established transportation companies have merged with Transport J.C. Germain to maximize its growth and efficiency.

As it stands, the company coordinates its activities from its head office in Trois-Rivières, its Montreal facility, and its Lennoxville-based sales team that serves its US customers.

Since 2020, the company has been operating under the name TFI Transport 1 Inc. and is proudly displaying its new JCG logo.

The success of the last few years also includes terminals in Trois-Rivières, Drummondville, Lennoxville, East Angus, Lachine, and Mississauga (Ontario), as well as storage and logistics, to position JCG as the carrier of choice for all your needs.

There is no doubt that the company's success stems from its ability to grasp its customers' needs and its will to provide simple, value-added solutions that minimize costs while fulfilling its commitments.

Company Short Description:
Alpine provides its clients with an interconnected Supply Network and adds value by lowering the Total Cost of Ownership of commodities and services. 

By concentrating all of the planning, ordering, tracking, delivery and inventory management in one specialized area, Alpine both improves efficiency and reduces overall cost for its clients.

As the supply chain of products and services becomes more complicated with evolved well designs and tighter supply of premium casing there is a requirement for more elaborate tubular supply chain management. As the supply chain looks like less the management of a 'commodity' the more of a fit Alpine becomes in the Oil and Gas industry. 

With focus on reducing our client's total cost of ownership through operational and planning efficiencies we have helped our clients save more than $20M since 2008. These savings have been derived from:

- Client owned inventory utilization and avoiding the purchase of new material
- Increased forecast accuracy that has lead to a greater spend with the primary mill supply 
- Surplus inventory disposition 
- Mill direct savings by eliminating distributor owned inventory 

While offering some of the more standard services attributed to the supply and management of tubular products and accessories: 

- Demand Forecasting 
- Procurement 
- Modifications (Slotting, Perforating etc)
- Logistics 
- Order Coordination 

Alpine offers services above and beyond the standard services that such as:

- The Industries only mill direct (transparent) supply solution 
- Supply chain reporting
- Client owned inventory management and disposition 
- Inspection, repair and re-utilization
- Vendor management

Contrary to a typical distribution, Alpine does not aim to service the mass market and supply material on a well by well basis but rather form long term strategic partnerships with their clients. With this alignment comes a combined effort to reduce the total cost of ownership of materials.
Headline: Owner & CEO At Alpine Tubular Management & Supply

Founded in 2008 by three high school friends in Windsor, Ontario, Green Processing Company Inc (GREEN) quickly became one of the premier packaging and recycling companies in North America.  

In a matter of a few years GREEN opened their first U.S.A location, in Cleveland, Ohio and expanded operations in Windsor.

The entrepreneurial spirit lead to the next expansion, this time in Laredo, Texas.  For the first time in their lives, the trio made their way to the lone star state. 

By 2013 GREEN was recycling over 2,000,000 lbs of plastic every month and repairing over 5,000 plastic containers.  

GREEN became a Tier 1 Automotive Supplier in 2014. A 50,000 square foot solar panel project was installed on the roof of their headquarters in Canada.

In 2016 it was time to board the plane (again) and head south, this time  to Alabama.  The management team was committed to opening a new location to bridge the service gap between Ontario and Texas. 

GREEN now owns and operates five company locations, made possible by the hard working and dedicated staff of 100 plus. Company Short Description:
GSH Mack/Volvo (anciennement Groupe Mack/Volvo). 

 Nous sommes un regroupement de  concessionnaire de Camions Mack et Volvo. Notre groupe compte 3 succursales, Laval, Les Coteaux et Montréal, pour assurer un service exceptionnelle, à proximité, pour l'ensemble de notre clientèle sur notre territoire.

Juin 2017 nous déménageons du boulevard Hymus vers une toute nouvelle succursale, bâtit en fonction des besoins de  notre entreprise.  Nous avons ouvert nos portes le 12 juin 2017 au 10475 chemin Cote de Liesse.

2014 est une année marquante pour nous.  Nous avons pris plusieurs initiatives afin d'améliorer nos services à notre clientèle en constante évolution, mais également pour mieux faire valoir nos marques Mack et Volvo.

Janvier 2014 à débuté en force avec le déménagement de notre succursale d'Anjou vers nos nouvelles installation de Laval. Celle-ci beaucoup plus spacieuse, adapté aux dernières technologies en plus de pouvoir desservir les clients qui possèdent des camions à carburant alternatif.

Nous avons profité de l'inauguration de cette nouvelle succursale pour mettre de l'avant nos marques, en nommant nos succursale en l'honneur de Mack et Volvo. La succursale de Montréal, actuel siège social du groupe, devient Camions Volvo Montréal et notre succursale de Laval est dorénavant Mack Laval. Camions Lourds de Montréal devient Le groupe Mack/Volvo. 

En juin 2014, nous avons ouvert notre troisième succursale à Les Coteaux, situé en bordure de l'autoroute 20 et à proximité de l'autoroute 30. Ce point de service sera une filiale de Camions Volvo Montréal.

Bien que nous ayons nommé nos succursales en l'honneur de chacune de nos marques, nous continuons à desservir en vente de camions, de pièces et de service les deux marques Mack et Volvo dans l'ensemble de nos succursales.

Nous n'avons qu'un seul objectif : Que chaque client qui entre chez nous en ressorte avec une expérience client 100% satisfaisant!

- Client owned inventory utilization and avoiding the purchase of new material
- Increased forecast accuracy that has lead to a greater spend with the primary mill supply

- Mill direct savings by eliminating distributor owned inventory 

While offering some of the more standard services attributed to the supply and management of tubular products and accessories: 

- Demand Forecasting 
- Procurement

- Logistics 
- Order Coordination 

Alpine offers services above and beyond the standard services that such as:

- The Industries only mill direct (transparent) supply solution 
- Supply chain reporting
- Client owned inventory management and disposition 
- Inspection, repair and re-utilization
- Vendor management

Contrary to a typical distribution, Alpine does not aim to service the mass market and supply material on a well by well basis but rather form long term strategic partnerships with their clients. With this alignment comes a combined effort to reduce the total cost of ownership of materials.

Company Short Description: The E-SMART system determines vehicle location in real time to actively manage the maximum speed that the truck can attain in the respective posted speed zone. The ability to govern the speed to each speed zone in real time allows for control of speed in all locations. The company explicitly targets clients involved in industries requiring cargo movement, storage, and handling at a port and rail interface, likely serving companies with significant shipping, import/export, or logistics needs. Company Short Description: Essex Terminal Railway is a short-line or switching railway, strategically located at the Windsor-Detroit gateway and busiest North American border crossing handling 1/3 of all US-Canada trade by volume. Incorporated in 1902, the ETR connects both local and internationally destined freight to CN, CP and CSX providing access to any point in the rail networks in Canada, the USA and Mexico. We also provide service for local companies from the east side of Windsor to Amherstburg, ON. ETR services customers in the industrial, chemical, agribusiness, salt, automotive engines, lumber, scrap and food products sectors. Our sister company, Morterm Ltd., owns and operates the only general cargo terminal in the Port of Windsor, with a rail siding to the terminal. The mainline of the ETR runs in a generally southwest direction for some 35 km (22 miles), in a route that roughly parallels the Detroit River separating Windsor from Detroit, MI, USA.
